stories.title: Deadline approaches for first Making Tax Digital quarterly update
stories.summary: HMRC has reminded Making Tax Digital for Income Tax customers that they have just two weeks left to submit their first mandatory quarterly update. This marks a key milestone in the rollout of Making Tax Digital for self-assessment taxpayers.

stories.title: Overseas workers conned out of thousands by fake care recruiter which never filed a single visa application
stories.summary: A bogus care recruitment company swindled overseas workers out of more than £19,000 by charging fees for visa sponsorship services it could not legally provide. The Home Office published the enforcement action as a warning to those seeking care work visas.

stories.title: Universities face ban on international students over visa compliance
stories.summary: UK Visas and Immigration has flagged that universities are facing bans on recruiting international students due to visa compliance failures. This marks a significant escalation in enforcement against higher education institutions.

stories.title: Electronic Monitoring Statistics — June 2026
stories.summary: The Home Office published the latest management information on the monitoring status of individuals with Electronic Monitoring orders in England and Wales for June 2026.

stories.title: FCDO statement: UN Integrated Food Security Phase Classification snapshot for Gaza
stories.summary: The Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office released an official statement on the UN IPC food security snapshot for Gaza, highlighting ongoing humanitarian concerns in the region.

stories.title: DWP to recommence transfer of ~150,000 adult DLA claimants to PIP reassessment
stories.summary: The Department for Work and Pensions announced it will restart the process of reassessing approximately 150,000 adult Disability Living Allowance (DLA) claimants for Personal Independence Payment (PIP), resuming a paused migration programme.

stories.title: Benefit overpayments reach £9.9 billion in 2025/26
stories.summary: The latest DWP figures reveal that benefit overpayments reached £9.9 billion in the financial year 2025/26, with Universal Credit accounting for the largest share at £6 billion. PIP overpayment rates also nearly doubled from 1.3% to 2.3%.

agencies.name: DEFRA
agencies.description: Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs

stories.title: Monthly statistics on UK hatcheries and poultry slaughterhouses — June 2026
stories.summary: DEFRA published June 2026 data showing UK commercial layer chick placings were down 6.1% at 3.5 million chicks, reflecting continued pressure in the poultry sector.

stories.title: Animal feed price statistics published — July 2026
stories.summary: DEFRA released its latest series of straights and compound animal feed prices across Great Britain, providing updated market data for farmers and agricultural businesses.

stories.title: MoD launches new female veterans toolkit
stories.summary: The Ministry of Defence has developed a new female veterans toolkit offering bespoke guidance and support, following concerns raised by Norfolk and Suffolk female veterans support groups about gaps in dedicated services.

stories.title: Reserve Forces' and Cadets' Associations annual report 2024–2025
stories.summary: The MoD published the annual report and audited accounts for the Reserve Forces' and Cadets' Associations for the financial year 1 April 2024 to 31 March 2025.

stories.title: UK DBT unveils new freight and aerospace plans with £100m Aerospace Supply Chain Fund
stories.summary: Business Secretary Jonathan Reynolds unveiled plans to harness innovation and drive growth across UK regions, including a new £100m Aerospace Supply Chain Fund to help suppliers scale up. The announcement supports PM Andy Burnham's pledge to reindustrialise Britain.

stories.title: India–UK Free Trade Agreement went live on 15 July 2026
stories.summary: The UK–India Free Trade Agreement, delivering zero-duty trade on a wide range of goods, officially went live on 15 July 2026, marking a major milestone for UK trade policy.

stories.title: DBT launches consultation on equal pay and pay discrimination reform
stories.summary: The government launched a consultation seeking views on a comprehensive reform of the current equal pay framework, aiming to make it fairer and more effective for workers across the UK.

agencies.name: DSIT
agencies.description: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

stories.title: PM Andy Burnham scraps DSIT, sending shockwaves through UK tech sector
stories.summary: Andy Burnham's new government announced plans to break up the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT), raising widespread concerns about the future of digital government and UK R&D policy. Industry groups warned the move could undermine years of work positioning the UK at the forefront of global science and technology.

stories.title: What impact will binning DSIT have on UK R&D?
stories.summary: Times Higher Education examines the implications of scrapping DSIT for UK research and development, with experts warning of policy uncertainty and loss of momentum in science investment.

stories.title: Health Secretary announces £4.5m for local maternity services
stories.summary: Health Secretary Yvette Cooper announced £4.5 million in funding for local maternity services during a visit to Royal Berkshire Hospital, emphasising the importance of high-quality maternity care across the country.

stories.title: Teenager becomes first in the UK to have pioneering spinal cord stimulation implant
stories.summary: A teenager at Newcastle Hospitals NHS Trust has become the first person in the UK to receive a spinal cord stimulation implant designed to disrupt pain signals travelling to the brain in real time, marking a significant medical breakthrough.

stories.title: Royal Mail warns of slower post due to Gibraltar border freight changes
stories.summary: Royal Mail issued a warning of postal delays as it switches from air to road transport (via Spain) for items to and from Gibraltar from 15 July, as part of work to ease border freight queues.

stories.title: Royal Mail to open 8,000 Post Office branches on British high streets in £43.9m deal
stories.summary: Royal Mail has announced plans to open 8,000 Post Office branches on British high streets for the first time as part of a major £43.9 million investment deal, significantly expanding the public-facing postal network.
